Remove NTP outbound access when instances have been configured with a time synchronization service and no longer need external NTP.

#!/bin/bash
# Cleanup: Revoke NTP access after sync is configured
APP_SG="sg-0a1b2c3d4e5f6g7h8"
REGION="us-east-1"

echo "Cleaning up NTP access rules..."
echo ""

# Revoke UDP port 123 (NTP) to public NTP servers
aws ec2 revoke-security-group-egress \
  --group-id $APP_SG \
  --protocol udp \
  --port 123 \
  --cidr 0.0.0.0/0 \
  --region $REGION

echo "✓ NTP access revoked"
echo ""
echo "Time synchronization configuration:"
echo "  Public NTP servers: BLOCKED"
echo "  Amazon Time Sync Service: Available"
echo "  Internal NTP: Not needed"
echo ""
echo "Note: Amazon Time Sync Service is available on 169.254.169.123"
echo "      (internal AWS service, doesn't require outbound rules)"
Cleaning up NTP access rules... { "Return": true, "SecurityGroupRules": [{ "GroupId": "sg-0a1b2c3d4e5f6g7h8", "SecurityGroupRuleId": "sgr-revoke-ntp", "IpProtocol": "udp", "FromPort": 123, "ToPort": 123, "CidrIpv4": "0.0.0.0/0", "IsEgress": true }] } ✓ NTP access revoked
# Revoke NTP access - time sync configured from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost from django.utils import timezone from datetime import datetime time_sync = { 'old_method': 'Public NTP servers', 'new_method': 'Amazon Time Sync Service', 'accuracy': 'Microsecond precision', 'status': 'CONFIGURED' } print(f"Time Synchronization Configuration") print(f"Old NTP source: {time_sync['old_method']}") print(f"New NTP source: {time_sync['new_method']}") print(f"") # Chronyd configuration before revoke before_config = { 'ntp_servers': ['pool.ntp.org', 'time.nist.gov'], 'sync_interval': '64 seconds', 'accuracy': '±100ms', 'status': 'Sync active' } print(f"Time sync configuration (before):") for key, value in before_config.items(): if isinstance(value, list): print(f" {key}:") for server in value: print(f" - {server}") else: print(f" {key}: {value}") print(f"") # After revocation configuration after_config = { 'ntp_servers': ['Amazon Time Sync (169.254.169.123)'], 'sync_interval': '60 seconds', 'accuracy': '±10ms (microseconds)', 'status': 'Sync active' } print(f"Time sync configuration (after):") for key, value in after_config.items(): if isinstance(value, list): print(f" {key}:") for server in value: print(f" - {server}") else: print(f" {key}: {value}") print(f"") # Clock accuracy impact accuracy_data = { 'drift_before': 150, 'drift_after': 5, 'improvement': 'Improved by 30x', 'database_replication': 'More reliable' } print(f"Time accuracy improvements:") for metric, value in accuracy_data.items(): if 'before' in metric: print(f" Clock drift before: {value}ms") elif 'after' in metric: print(f" Clock drift after: {value}ms") else: print(f" {metric}: {value}") print(f"") # Applications using accurate time time_dependent_features = { 'token_expiration': 'JWT/session tokens', 'database_replication': 'MySQL binlog timestamps', 'log_aggregation': 'CloudWatch log ordering', 'scheduled_tasks': 'Celery task timing', 'rate_limiting': 'Token bucket algorithms' } print(f"Features depending on accurate time:") for feature, dependency in time_dependent_features.items(): print(f" ✓ {dependency}") # NTP server endpoints ntp_info = { 'service': 'Amazon Time Sync Service', 'endpoint': '169.254.169.123:123', 'scope': 'Internal (no internet routing)', 'security_group': 'Not required' } print(f"\nAmazon Time Sync details:") for key, value in ntp_info.items(): print(f" {key}: {value}") print(f"\n✓ NTP outbound rules cleaned up") print(f"✓ Time sync fully configured")
Example 5: Revoke All Egress Rules for Forensic Isolation

Remove all outbound rules during forensic investigation to prevent any communication while analyzing a potentially compromised instance.

#!/bin/bash
# Forensic isolation: Revoke all outbound access
COMPROMISED_SG="sg-5e6f7g8h9i0j1k2l3"
REGION="us-east-1"

echo "🔒 FORENSIC ISOLATION IN PROGRESS"
echo "Revoking all outbound access..."
echo ""

# Revoke all protocols (tcp, udp, icmp, all)
aws ec2 revoke-security-group-egress \
  --group-id $COMPROMISED_SG \
  --protocol -1 \
  --cidr 0.0.0.0/0 \
  --region $REGION

echo "✓ All outbound rules revoked"
echo ""
echo "Instance isolation status:"
echo "  Internet access: BLOCKED"
echo "  Internal VPC access: BLOCKED"
echo "  All outbound: BLOCKED"
echo ""
echo "Forensic investigation:"
echo "  Instance state: ISOLATED"
echo "  SSH access: Via Systems Manager Session Manager"
echo "  Data collection: Via EBS snapshot"
echo "  Next steps: Forensic analysis of logs/memory"
🔒 FORENSIC ISOLATION IN PROGRESS Revoking all outbound access... { "Return": true, "SecurityGroupRules": [{ "GroupId": "sg-5e6f7g8h9i0j1k2l3", "SecurityGroupRuleId": "sgr-revoke-all-egress", "IpProtocol": "-1", "FromPort": 0, "ToPort": 65535, "CidrIpv4": "0.0.0.0/0", "IsEgress": true }] } ✓ All outbound rules revoked
# Forensic isolation - revoke all outbound from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost from django.utils import timezone from datetime import datetime forensic_case = { 'case_id': 'FOR-2026-0042', 'severity': 'CRITICAL', 'isolation_time': '2026-01-11 16:30 UTC', 'status': 'ISOLATED' } print(f"Forensic Investigation - Complete Isolation") print(f"Case ID: {forensic_case['case_id']}") print(f"Severity: {forensic_case['severity']}") print(f"Isolation time: {forensic_case['isolation_time']}") print(f"") # Instance isolation details isolation_status = { 'outbound_to_internet': 'BLOCKED', 'outbound_to_vpc': 'BLOCKED', 'outbound_to_database': 'BLOCKED', 'inbound_ssh': 'Via Session Manager only', 'disk_access': 'Read/snapshot available', 'network_status': 'ISOLATED' } print(f"Instance isolation status:") for service, status in isolation_status.items(): print(f" {service}: {status}") print(f"") # Forensic data collection plan forensic_plan = { 'ebs_snapshot': 'Capture system disk', 'memory_dump': 'Analyze via Systems Manager', 'network_logs': 'Query VPC Flow Logs', 'cloudtrail_logs': 'Review API calls', 'cloudwatch_logs': 'Application logs analysis' } print(f"Forensic data collection:") for data_source, purpose in forensic_plan.items(): print(f" - {data_source}: {purpose}") print(f"") # Investigation procedures investigation = { 'step1': 'Snapshot instance disk', 'step2': 'Dump memory contents', 'step3': 'Analyze process list', 'step4': 'Review network connections', 'step5': 'Examine log files', 'step6': 'Malware detection scan', 'step7': 'Timeline reconstruction' } print(f"Investigation steps:") for step, action in investigation.items(): print(f" {step}: {action}") print(f"") # Communication during isolation communication = { 'ssh_remote': 'BLOCKED (prevent evidence tampering)', 'session_manager': 'AVAILABLE (secure shell)', 'ebs_snapshot': 'AVAILABLE (read-only copy)', 'ssm_agent': 'Active for access only' } print(f"Communication methods during isolation:") for method, availability in communication.items(): print(f" {method}: {availability}") # Potential threats detected threats = { 'suspicious_process': 'Mining software detected', 'outbound_connections': 'C2 traffic attempted', 'unauthorized_users': 'Shell history modified', 'privilege_escalation': 'Sudo logs tampered' } print(f"\nDetected threats:") for threat_type, detail in threats.items(): print(f" ⚠ {threat_type}: {detail}") # Timeline reconstruction timeline = { 'initial_entry': '2026-01-10 03:15 UTC', 'lateral_movement': '2026-01-10 04:22 UTC', 'c2_communication': '2026-01-10 06:45 UTC', 'alert_triggered': '2026-01-11 16:20 UTC', 'isolation_completed': '2026-01-11 16:30 UTC' } print(f"\nIncident timeline:") for event, time in timeline.items(): print(f" {event}: {time}") print(f"\n✓ Instance forensically isolated") print(f"✓ Investigation in progress")

create-vpc

Create a Virtual Private Cloud (VPC) - an isolated network environment for your AWS infrastructure. A VPC is the foundation for deploying applications with complete control over IP addressing, subnets, routing, and security.

Syntax
aws ec2 create-vpc \
  --cidr-block  \
  [--tag-specifications 'ResourceType=vpc,Tags=[{Key=Name,Value=}]'] \
  [--region ]
Parameters Description
  • --cidr-block (string): The IPv4 address range for the VPC in CIDR notation (e.g., 10.0.0.0/16). This is the primary IP address block for the VPC. Choose /16 for large deployments or /20 for smaller ones.
  • --tag-specifications (string): Tags to apply to the VPC for organization and billing. Format: ResourceType=vpc,Tags=[{Key=Name,Value=my-vpc}]
  • --amazon-provided-ipv6-cidr-block (boolean): Request an IPv6 CIDR block for the VPC (optional, for IPv6 support)
  • --region (string): AWS region where the VPC is created (us-east-1, eu-west-1, etc.)
  • --enable-dns-hostnames (boolean): Enable DNS hostnames in the VPC (useful for application domain names)
  • --enable-dns-support (boolean): Enable DNS support to allow instances to resolve domain names
Return Value
  • Vpc (Object): The created VPC object with detailed information
  • VpcId (String): Unique VPC identifier (vpc-xxxxxxxx)
  • CidrBlock (String): The IPv4 CIDR block assigned to the VPC
  • State (String): Current VPC state (pending, available)
  • IsDefault (Boolean): True if this is the default VPC, false for custom VPCs
  • Tags (Array): Tags applied to the VPC for identification
  • OwnerId (String): AWS account ID that owns the VPC
Example 1: Create Production VPC with /16 CIDR Block

Create a production VPC with a large IP address space (/16) to accommodate multiple subnets across availability zones.

#!/bin/bash
# Create production VPC for Django application
CIDR_BLOCK="10.0.0.0/16"
REGION="us-east-1"
VPC_NAME="anihpj-production-vpc"

echo "Creating production VPC..."
echo "CIDR Block: $CIDR_BLOCK"
echo ""

# Create VPC with tags
VPC_ID=$(aws ec2 create-vpc \
  --cidr-block $CIDR_BLOCK \
  --tag-specifications "ResourceType=vpc,Tags=[{Key=Name,Value=$VPC_NAME},{Key=Environment,Value=production}]" \
  --region $REGION \
  --query 'Vpc.VpcId' \
  --output text)

echo "✓ VPC created"
echo "VPC ID: $VPC_ID"
echo ""

# Enable DNS support
aws ec2 modify-vpc-attribute \
  --vpc-id $VPC_ID \
  --enable-dns-hostnames \
  --region $REGION

echo "✓ DNS hostnames enabled"
echo ""
echo "VPC Configuration:"
echo "  VPC ID: $VPC_ID"
echo "  CIDR Block: $CIDR_BLOCK"
echo "  Available subnets: 256 (with /24 subnets)"
echo "  IP address space: 65,536 IPs"
Creating production VPC... CIDR Block: 10.0.0.0/16 { "Vpc": { "CidrBlock": "10.0.0.0/16", "DhcpOptionsId": "dopt-12345678", "State": "available", "VpcId": "vpc-0a1b2c3d4e5f6g7h8", "OwnerId": "123456789012", "IsDefault": false, "Tags": [{ "Key": "Name", "Value": "anihpj-production-vpc" }] } } ✓ VPC created VPC ID: vpc-0a1b2c3d4e5f6g7h8 ✓ DNS hostnames enabled
# Create production VPC from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost from django.utils import timezone vpc_config = { 'vpc_id': 'vpc-0a1b2c3d4e5f6g7h8', 'vpc_name': 'anihpj-production-vpc', 'cidr_block': '10.0.0.0/16', 'environment': 'production', 'region': 'us-east-1' } print(f"Production VPC Created") print(f"VPC ID: {vpc_config['vpc_id']}") print(f"Name: {vpc_config['vpc_name']}") print(f"CIDR Block: {vpc_config['cidr_block']}") print(f"Environment: {vpc_config['environment']}") print(f"") # VPC address space allocation address_space = { 'total_ips': 65536, 'usable_ips': 65531, 'reserved_ips': 5, 'available_subnets': 256 } print(f"IP Address space allocation:") print(f" Total IPs: {address_space['total_ips']}") print(f" Usable IPs: {address_space['usable_ips']}") print(f" Reserved: {address_space['reserved_ips']}") print(f"") # Subnet planning for multi-AZ deployment subnets_planned = { 'public_1a': {'cidr': '10.0.1.0/24', 'az': 'us-east-1a', 'type': 'Public'}, 'public_1b': {'cidr': '10.0.2.0/24', 'az': 'us-east-1b', 'type': 'Public'}, 'private_app_1a': {'cidr': '10.0.10.0/24', 'az': 'us-east-1a', 'type': 'Private'}, 'private_app_1b': {'cidr': '10.0.11.0/24', 'az': 'us-east-1b', 'type': 'Private'}, 'private_db_1a': {'cidr': '10.0.20.0/24', 'az': 'us-east-1a', 'type': 'Private'}, 'private_db_1b': {'cidr': '10.0.21.0/24', 'az': 'us-east-1b', 'type': 'Private'} } print(f"Planned subnet topology (multi-AZ):") for subnet_name, subnet_config in list(subnets_planned.items())[:3]: print(f" {subnet_name}: {subnet_config['cidr']} ({subnet_config['type']})") # VPC resources summary resources = { 'vpc': 1, 'subnets': 0, 'route_tables': 1, 'nat_gateways': 0, 'internet_gateways': 0, 'security_groups': 1 } print(f"\nInitial VPC resources:") for resource, count in resources.items(): print(f" {resource}: {count}") # High availability configuration ha_config = { 'availability_zones': 2, 'nat_gateways_per_az': 1, 'instances_per_az': 3, 'multi_az_rds': True } print(f"\nHigh Availability configuration:") print(f" Availability zones: {ha_config['availability_zones']}") print(f" NAT gateways: {ha_config['nat_gateways_per_az']} per AZ") print(f" Multi-AZ RDS: {ha_config['multi_az_rds']}") # DNS configuration dns_config = { 'dns_hostnames': True, 'dns_support': True, 'route53_integration': True, 'internal_domain': 'internal.anihpj.aws' } print(f"\nDNS Configuration:") for setting, value in dns_config.items(): print(f" {setting}: {value}") print(f"\n✓ Production VPC ready for subnets") print(f"✓ High availability configured")
Example 2: Create Development VPC with /20 CIDR Block

Create a smaller development VPC with /20 CIDR block (4,096 IPs) for development and testing environments.

#!/bin/bash
# Create development VPC with smaller address space
CIDR_BLOCK="10.100.0.0/20"
REGION="us-east-1"
VPC_NAME="anihpj-dev-vpc"

echo "Creating development VPC..."
echo "CIDR Block: $CIDR_BLOCK (4,096 IPs)"
echo ""

# Create development VPC
VPC_ID=$(aws ec2 create-vpc \
  --cidr-block $CIDR_BLOCK \
  --tag-specifications "ResourceType=vpc,Tags=[{Key=Name,Value=$VPC_NAME},{Key=Environment,Value=development},{Key=CostCenter,Value=engineering}]" \
  --region $REGION \
  --query 'Vpc.VpcId' \
  --output text)

echo "✓ VPC created"
echo "VPC ID: $VPC_ID"
echo ""

# Enable DNS support
aws ec2 modify-vpc-attribute \
  --vpc-id $VPC_ID \
  --enable-dns-hostnames \
  --region $REGION

echo "✓ DNS hostnames enabled"
echo ""
echo "Development VPC Configuration:"
echo "  VPC ID: $VPC_ID"
echo "  CIDR Block: $CIDR_BLOCK"
echo "  IP address space: 4,096 IPs"
echo "  Cost: Lower (smaller address space)"
echo "  Use case: Testing, staging, feature development"
Creating development VPC... CIDR Block: 10.100.0.0/20 (4,096 IPs) { "Vpc": { "CidrBlock": "10.100.0.0/20", "DhcpOptionsId": "dopt-87654321", "State": "available", "VpcId": "vpc-1b2c3d4e5f6g7h8i9", "OwnerId": "123456789012", "IsDefault": false, "Tags": [{ "Key": "Name", "Value": "anihpj-dev-vpc" }] } } ✓ VPC created VPC ID: vpc-1b2c3d4e5f6g7h8i9 ✓ DNS hostnames enabled
# Create development VPC from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost from django.utils import timezone dev_vpc_config = { 'vpc_id': 'vpc-1b2c3d4e5f6g7h8i9', 'vpc_name': 'anihpj-dev-vpc', 'cidr_block': '10.100.0.0/20', 'environment': 'development', 'region': 'us-east-1' } print(f"Development VPC Created") print(f"VPC ID: {dev_vpc_config['vpc_id']}") print(f"Name: {dev_vpc_config['vpc_name']}") print(f"CIDR Block: {dev_vpc_config['cidr_block']}") print(f"Environment: {dev_vpc_config['environment']}") print(f"") # Dev VPC address space dev_address_space = { 'total_ips': 4096, 'usable_ips': 4091, 'reserved_ips': 5, 'available_subnets': 16 } print(f"IP Address space allocation:") print(f" Total IPs: {dev_address_space['total_ips']}") print(f" Usable IPs: {dev_address_space['usable_ips']}") print(f" Available /24 subnets: {dev_address_space['available_subnets']}") print(f"") # Development environment layout dev_subnets = { 'dev_public': {'cidr': '10.100.1.0/24', 'purpose': 'Bastion hosts, NAT'}, 'dev_app': {'cidr': '10.100.2.0/24', 'purpose': 'Django dev instances'}, 'dev_db': {'cidr': '10.100.3.0/24', 'purpose': 'MySQL dev database'}, 'dev_cache': {'cidr': '10.100.4.0/24', 'purpose': 'Redis dev cache'} } print(f"Development subnet plan:") for subnet_name, subnet_info in dev_subnets.items(): print(f" {subnet_name}: {subnet_info['cidr']}") print(f" Purpose: {subnet_info['purpose']}") # Development resources dev_resources = { 'instances': 5, 'databases': 1, 'caches': 1, 'load_balancers': 1, 'cost_estimate_monthly': 150 } print(f"\nDevelopment environment resources:") for resource, count in dev_resources.items(): if resource != 'cost_estimate_monthly': print(f" {resource}: {count}") print(f"") print(f"Estimated monthly cost: ${dev_resources['cost_estimate_monthly']}") # Development team access dev_team = { 'developers': 8, 'qa_engineers': 3, 'devops': 2 } print(f"\nDevelopment team size:") for role, count in dev_team.items(): print(f" {role}: {count}") # Development workflow dev_workflow = { 'feature_branches': 'Isolated in this VPC', 'merge_to_main': 'Triggers staging deployment', 'release_cycle': 'Weekly releases', 'testing': 'Full integration tests' } print(f"\nDevelopment workflow:") for step, description in dev_workflow.items(): print(f" {step}: {description}") print(f"\n✓ Development VPC ready") print(f"✓ Team environments configured")
Example 3: Create VPC with Custom Tags for Multi-Environment Setup

Create a VPC with comprehensive tags for cost allocation, automation, and environment management across production and non-production.

#!/bin/bash
# Create VPC with comprehensive tagging for cost tracking
CIDR_BLOCK="10.50.0.0/16"
REGION="us-east-1"

echo "Creating VPC with comprehensive tags..."
echo ""

VPC_ID=$(aws ec2 create-vpc \
  --cidr-block $CIDR_BLOCK \
  --tag-specifications "ResourceType=vpc,Tags=[
    {Key=Name,Value=anihpj-staging-vpc},
    {Key=Environment,Value=staging},
    {Key=Application,Value=jobpost},
    {Key=Team,Value=backend},
    {Key=CostCenter,Value=engineering},
    {Key=Owner,Value=devops-team},
    {Key=BackupPolicy,Value=daily},
    {Key=ChangeControl,Value=required}
  ]" \
  --region $REGION \
  --query 'Vpc.VpcId' \
  --output text)

echo "✓ VPC created with tags"
echo "VPC ID: $VPC_ID"
echo ""

# List all tags
aws ec2 describe-tags \
  --filters "Name=resource-id,Values=$VPC_ID" \
  --region $REGION \
  --query 'Tags[*].[Key,Value]' \
  --output table

echo ""
echo "Tagging strategy benefits:"
echo "  Cost allocation by team and application"
echo "  Automated resource management"
echo "  Environment isolation and compliance"
echo "  Backup and disaster recovery policies"
Creating VPC with comprehensive tags... { "Vpc": { "CidrBlock": "10.50.0.0/16", "VpcId": "vpc-2c3d4e5f6g7h8i9j0", "State": "available", "OwnerId": "123456789012", "Tags": [ {Key: Name, Value: anihpj-staging-vpc}, {Key: Environment, Value: staging}, {Key: Application, Value: jobpost} ] } } ✓ VPC created with tags VPC ID: vpc-2c3d4e5f6g7h8i9j0 Tags applied: +-------------------+-------------------+ | Key | Value | +-------------------+-------------------+ | Name | anihpj-staging-vpc| | Environment | staging | | Application | jobpost | | Team | backend | | CostCenter | engineering | | Owner | devops-team | | BackupPolicy | daily | | ChangeControl | required | +-------------------+-------------------+
# Create VPC with comprehensive tagging from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost from django.utils import timezone tagged_vpc = { 'vpc_id': 'vpc-2c3d4e5f6g7h8i9j0', 'vpc_name': 'anihpj-staging-vpc', 'environment': 'staging', 'cidr_block': '10.50.0.0/16' } print(f"VPC Created with Comprehensive Tags") print(f"VPC ID: {tagged_vpc['vpc_id']}") print(f"Name: {tagged_vpc['vpc_name']}") print(f"Environment: {tagged_vpc['environment']}") print(f"") # Tagging strategy tags_applied = { 'Name': 'anihpj-staging-vpc', 'Environment': 'staging', 'Application': 'jobpost', 'Team': 'backend', 'CostCenter': 'engineering', 'Owner': 'devops-team', 'BackupPolicy': 'daily', 'ChangeControl': 'required' } print(f"Tags applied to VPC:") for tag_key, tag_value in tags_applied.items(): print(f" {tag_key}: {tag_value}") print(f"") # Cost tracking by tags cost_allocation = { 'engineering_team': 850, 'application_jobpost': 2100, 'staging_environment': 1400, 'monthly_total': 4350 } print(f"Cost allocation (monthly):") print(f" Engineering team: ${cost_allocation['engineering_team']}") print(f" JobPost application: ${cost_allocation['application_jobpost']}") print(f" Staging environment: ${cost_allocation['staging_environment']}") print(f" Total: ${cost_allocation['monthly_total']}") print(f"") # Automation and compliance automation = { 'auto_tagging': 'Enforced via IAM policy', 'backup_policy': 'Snapshots every 24 hours', 'change_control': 'Requires approval for changes', 'cost_alerts': 'Trigger at $5000/month' } print(f"Automation and governance:") for policy, detail in automation.items(): print(f" {policy}: {detail}") # Multi-environment isolation environments = { 'production': 'vpc-0a1b2c3d4e5f6g7h8', 'staging': 'vpc-2c3d4e5f6g7h8i9j0', 'development': 'vpc-1b2c3d4e5f6g7h8i9', 'disaster_recovery': 'vpc-3d4e5f6g7h8i9j0k1' } print(f"\nMulti-environment setup:") for env, vpc_id in environments.items(): print(f" {env}: {vpc_id}") # Resource management resource_policies = { 'backup': 'Daily snapshots, 30-day retention', 'monitoring': 'CloudWatch metrics enabled', 'logging': 'VPC Flow Logs enabled', 'patching': 'Auto-patching enabled' } print(f"\nResource management policies:") for policy, setting in resource_policies.items(): print(f" {policy}: {setting}") print(f"\n✓ VPC with tags created") print(f"✓ Cost allocation configured")
Example 4: Create VPC for Multi-Region Deployment

Create VPCs in multiple regions for disaster recovery and geographic distribution of the Django application.

#!/bin/bash
# Create VPCs in multiple regions for DR and geo-distribution
declare -A regions=(
  ["us-east-1"]="10.0.0.0/16"
  ["eu-west-1"]="10.1.0.0/16"
  ["ap-southeast-1"]="10.2.0.0/16"
)

echo "Creating VPCs for multi-region deployment..."
echo ""

for region in "${!regions[@]}"; do
  cidr=${regions[$region]}
  vpc_name="anihpj-vpc-$region"
  
  echo "Creating VPC in $region: $cidr"
  
  vpc_id=$(aws ec2 create-vpc \
    --cidr-block $cidr \
    --tag-specifications "ResourceType=vpc,Tags=[
      {Key=Name,Value=$vpc_name},
      {Key=Environment,Value=production},
      {Key=Region,Value=$region},
      {Key=DisasterRecovery,Value=enabled}
    ]" \
    --region $region \
    --query 'Vpc.VpcId' \
    --output text)
  
  echo "  VPC ID: $vpc_id"
  
  # Enable DNS
  aws ec2 modify-vpc-attribute \
    --vpc-id $vpc_id \
    --enable-dns-hostnames \
    --region $region
  
  echo "  ✓ DNS enabled"
  echo ""
done

echo "Multi-region VPC deployment complete"
echo "VPCs ready for peering and replication"
Creating VPCs for multi-region deployment... Creating VPC in us-east-1: 10.0.0.0/16 { "Vpc": { "VpcId": "vpc-0a1b2c3d4e5f6g7h8", "CidrBlock": "10.0.0.0/16", "State": "available" } } VPC ID: vpc-0a1b2c3d4e5f6g7h8 ✓ DNS enabled Creating VPC in eu-west-1: 10.1.0.0/16 { "Vpc": { "VpcId": "vpc-1b2c3d4e5f6g7h8i9", "CidrBlock": "10.1.0.0/16", "State": "available" } } VPC ID: vpc-1b2c3d4e5f6g7h8i9 ✓ DNS enabled Multi-region VPC deployment complete
# Create VPCs for multi-region deployment from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost from django.utils import timezone multi_region_vpcs = { 'us-east-1': { 'vpc_id': 'vpc-0a1b2c3d4e5f6g7h8', 'cidr': '10.0.0.0/16', 'purpose': 'Primary production' }, 'eu-west-1': { 'vpc_id': 'vpc-1b2c3d4e5f6g7h8i9', 'cidr': '10.1.0.0/16', 'purpose': 'Europe production' }, 'ap-southeast-1': { 'vpc_id': 'vpc-2c3d4e5f6g7h8i9j0', 'cidr': '10.2.0.0/16', 'purpose': 'Asia-Pacific production' } } print(f"Multi-Region VPC Deployment") print(f"") for region, vpc_info in multi_region_vpcs.items(): print(f"{region}:") print(f" VPC ID: {vpc_info['vpc_id']}") print(f" CIDR: {vpc_info['cidr']}") print(f" Purpose: {vpc_info['purpose']}") print(f"") # Global infrastructure topology infrastructure = { 'regions': 3, 'vpcs': 3, 'availability_zones': 6, 'data_centers': 6 } print(f"Global infrastructure:") for component, count in infrastructure.items(): print(f" {component}: {count}") print(f"") # Disaster recovery configuration dr_config = { 'primary_region': 'us-east-1', 'dr_region': 'eu-west-1', 'rto_hours': 1, 'rpo_minutes': 15, 'data_replication': 'Continuous (RDS Multi-Region)' } print(f"Disaster Recovery configuration:") for setting, value in dr_config.items(): print(f" {setting}: {value}") print(f"") # Geographic distribution benefits distribution_benefits = { 'latency_reduction': 'Serve users from nearest region', 'compliance': 'Data residency requirements (GDPR)', 'reliability': 'Independent failure domains', 'performance': 'Sub-100ms latency globally' } print(f"Geographic distribution benefits:") for benefit, description in distribution_benefits.items(): print(f" ✓ {benefit}: {description}") # VPC Peering for inter-region communication peering_config = { 'us_east_to_eu': 'vpc-0a1b2c3d4e5f6g7h8 ↔ vpc-1b2c3d4e5f6g7h8i9', 'us_east_to_ap': 'vpc-0a1b2c3d4e5f6g7h8 ↔ vpc-2c3d4e5f6g7h8i9j0', 'eu_to_ap': 'vpc-1b2c3d4e5f6g7h8i9 ↔ vpc-2c3d4e5f6g7h8i9j0' } print(f"\nVPC Peering connections (planned):") for peering, vpcs in peering_config.items(): print(f" {peering}") # Multi-region database replication db_replication = { 'primary': 'us-east-1 (RDS Master)', 'read_replica_1': 'eu-west-1 (Same-region read)', 'read_replica_2': 'ap-southeast-1 (Same-region read)', 'cross_region_replica': 'eu-west-1 (Disaster recovery)', 'replication_lag': '< 1 second' } print(f"\nDatabase replication:") for role, config in db_replication.items(): print(f" {role}: {config}") # User distribution and routing user_routing = { 'north_america': 'Route to us-east-1', 'europe': 'Route to eu-west-1', 'asia_pacific': 'Route to ap-southeast-1', 'routing_method': 'Route53 geolocation routing' } print(f"\nUser routing strategy:") for region, routing in user_routing.items(): print(f" {region}: {routing}") print(f"\n✓ Multi-region infrastructure deployed") print(f"✓ Global application ready")
Example 5: Create VPC with Secondary CIDR Block for Expansion

Create a VPC with multiple CIDR blocks to support future expansion without recreating the VPC.

#!/bin/bash
# Create VPC with primary CIDR, plan for secondary CIDR expansion
PRIMARY_CIDR="10.0.0.0/16"
SECONDARY_CIDR="10.1.0.0/16"
REGION="us-east-1"
VPC_NAME="anihpj-expandable-vpc"

echo "Creating VPC with expansion capability..."
echo "Primary CIDR: $PRIMARY_CIDR"
echo "Secondary CIDR (for future): $SECONDARY_CIDR"
echo ""

# Create VPC with primary CIDR
VPC_ID=$(aws ec2 create-vpc \
  --cidr-block $PRIMARY_CIDR \
  --tag-specifications "ResourceType=vpc,Tags=[
    {Key=Name,Value=$VPC_NAME},
    {Key=Environment,Value=production},
    {Key=ExpansionCapability,Value=enabled}
  ]" \
  --region $REGION \
  --query 'Vpc.VpcId' \
  --output text)

echo "✓ Primary VPC created"
echo "VPC ID: $VPC_ID"
echo ""

# Associate secondary CIDR (optional, for future expansion)
echo "Adding secondary CIDR for future expansion..."
aws ec2 associate-vpc-cidr-block \
  --vpc-id $VPC_ID \
  --cidr-block $SECONDARY_CIDR \
  --region $REGION

echo "✓ Secondary CIDR associated"
echo ""

# Enable DNS
aws ec2 modify-vpc-attribute \
  --vpc-id $VPC_ID \
  --enable-dns-hostnames \
  --region $REGION

echo "VPC Configuration Summary:"
echo "  VPC ID: $VPC_ID"
echo "  Primary CIDR: $PRIMARY_CIDR (65,536 IPs)"
echo "  Secondary CIDR: $SECONDARY_CIDR (65,536 IPs)"
echo "  Total available: 131,072 IPs"
echo "  Expansion ready: YES"
Creating VPC with expansion capability... Primary CIDR: 10.0.0.0/16 Secondary CIDR: 10.1.0.0/16 ✓ Primary VPC created VPC ID: vpc-3d4e5f6g7h8i9j0k1 { "Vpc": { "VpcId": "vpc-3d4e5f6g7h8i9j0k1", "CidrBlock": "10.0.0.0/16", "CidrBlockAssociationSet": [ { "CidrBlock": "10.0.0.0/16", "AssociationId": "vpc-cidr-assoc-0a1b2c3d", "CidrBlockState": "associated" } ], "State": "available" } } ✓ Secondary CIDR associated { "Ipv6CidrBlockAssociation": { "AssociationId": "vpc-cidr-assoc-1a2b3c4d", "Ipv6CidrBlock": "10.1.0.0/16", "Ipv6CidrBlockState": "associated" } } VPC Configuration Summary
# Create VPC with secondary CIDR for expansion from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost from django.utils import timezone expandable_vpc = { 'vpc_id': 'vpc-3d4e5f6g7h8i9j0k1', 'primary_cidr': '10.0.0.0/16', 'secondary_cidr': '10.1.0.0/16', 'status': 'Ready for expansion' } print(f"Expandable VPC Configuration") print(f"VPC ID: {expandable_vpc['vpc_id']}") print(f"Status: {expandable_vpc['status']}") print(f"") # CIDR block allocation cidr_allocation = { 'primary': { 'cidr': '10.0.0.0/16', 'total_ips': 65536, 'purpose': 'Current production', 'utilization': 45 }, 'secondary': { 'cidr': '10.1.0.0/16', 'total_ips': 65536, 'purpose': 'Future expansion', 'utilization': 0 } } print(f"CIDR block allocation:") for cidr_type, config in cidr_allocation.items(): print(f" {cidr_type}:") print(f" CIDR: {config['cidr']}") print(f" Total IPs: {config['total_ips']}") print(f" Utilization: {config['utilization']}%") print(f"") # Capacity planning capacity = { 'current_subnets': 8, 'max_subnets': 16, 'current_ips_used': 29491, 'available_ips': 101045, 'growth_runway': '2+ years' } print(f"Capacity planning:") print(f" Current IP usage: {capacity['current_ips_used']} IPs") print(f" Available IPs: {capacity['available_ips']}") print(f" Growth runway: {capacity['growth_runway']}") print(f" Expansion capability: UNLIMITED") print(f"") # Expansion timeline expansion_phases = { 'phase_1': 'Initial deployment (10.0.0.0/16)', 'phase_2': 'Add secondary CIDR (10.1.0.0/16) - Year 2', 'phase_3': 'Scale to 3 secondary CIDR blocks - Year 3' } print(f"Expansion phases:") for phase, description in expansion_phases.items(): print(f" {phase}: {description}") # Instance distribution instance_distribution = { 'current_instances': 15, 'max_instances_primary': 500, 'max_instances_secondary': 500, 'total_capacity': 1000 } print(f"\nInstance capacity:") print(f" Current instances: {instance_distribution['current_instances']}") print(f" Total capacity: {instance_distribution['total_capacity']}") print(f" Utilization: {(instance_distribution['current_instances']/instance_distribution['total_capacity']*100):.1f}%") # Future features enabled future_capabilities = { 'multiple_cidrs': True, 'ipv6_support': False, 'transit_gateway': True, 'vpc_peering': True } print(f"\nFuture capabilities enabled:") for capability, enabled in future_capabilities.items(): status = '✓' if enabled else '✗' print(f" {status} {capability}") print(f"\n✓ Expandable VPC ready") print(f"✓ Growth accommodated for years ahead")

create-subnet

Create a subnet within a VPC. Subnets divide your VPC into smaller network segments for organizing resources and controlling network traffic. Each subnet is associated with a single availability zone and can be public (internet-routable) or private (internal only).

Syntax
aws ec2 create-subnet \
  --vpc-id  \
  --cidr-block  \
  [--availability-zone ] \
  [--tag-specifications 'ResourceType=subnet,Tags=[{Key=Name,Value=}]'] \
  [--region ]
Parameters Description
  • --vpc-id (string): The ID of the VPC where the subnet will be created. Format: vpc-xxxxxxxx
  • --cidr-block (string): The IPv4 CIDR block for the subnet in CIDR notation (e.g., 10.0.1.0/24). Must be a subset of the VPC's CIDR block.
  • --availability-zone (string): The availability zone where the subnet will be created (us-east-1a, us-east-1b, etc.). Omit to let AWS choose.
  • --tag-specifications (string): Tags to apply to the subnet. Format: ResourceType=subnet,Tags=[{Key=Name,Value=my-subnet}]
  • --region (string): AWS region where the VPC is located (us-east-1, eu-west-1, etc.)
  • --ipv6-cidr-block (string): IPv6 CIDR block for the subnet (optional, for IPv6 support)
Return Value
  • Subnet (Object): The created subnet object with detailed information
  • SubnetId (String): Unique subnet identifier (subnet-xxxxxxxx)
  • VpcId (String): VPC containing the subnet
  • CidrBlock (String): The IPv4 CIDR block assigned to the subnet
  • AvailabilityZone (String): Availability zone where the subnet is located
  • State (String): Current subnet state (pending, available)
  • AvailableIpAddressCount (Integer): Number of available IP addresses in the subnet
  • Tags (Array): Tags applied to the subnet for identification
Example 1: Create Public Subnet for Web Tier

Create a public subnet in the first availability zone for hosting web servers and load balancers.

#!/bin/bash
# Create public subnet for web tier
VPC_ID="vpc-0a1b2c3d4e5f6g7h8"
SUBNET_CIDR="10.0.1.0/24"
AZ="us-east-1a"
REGION="us-east-1"
SUBNET_NAME="anihpj-public-web-1a"

echo "Creating public subnet for web tier..."
echo "VPC ID: $VPC_ID"
echo "CIDR: $SUBNET_CIDR"
echo "AZ: $AZ"
echo ""

# Create the public subnet
SUBNET_ID=$(aws ec2 create-subnet \
  --vpc-id $VPC_ID \
  --cidr-block $SUBNET_CIDR \
  --availability-zone $AZ \
  --tag-specifications "ResourceType=subnet,Tags=[
    {Key=Name,Value=$SUBNET_NAME},
    {Key=Type,Value=Public},
    {Key=Layer,Value=Web}
  ]" \
  --region $REGION \
  --query 'Subnet.SubnetId' \
  --output text)

echo "✓ Subnet created"
echo "Subnet ID: $SUBNET_ID"
echo ""

# Enable auto-assign public IP
aws ec2 modify-subnet-attribute \
  --subnet-id $SUBNET_ID \
  --map-public-ip-on-launch \
  --region $REGION

echo "✓ Auto-assign public IP enabled"
echo ""
echo "Subnet details:"
echo "  Type: Public (routes to Internet Gateway)"
echo "  Layer: Web tier (ALB, web servers)"
echo "  Available IPs: 256 (minus 5 reserved)"
Creating public subnet for web tier... VPC ID: vpc-0a1b2c3d4e5f6g7h8 CIDR: 10.0.1.0/24 AZ: us-east-1a { "Subnet": { "SubnetId": "subnet-0a1b2c3d4e5f6g7h", "VpcId": "vpc-0a1b2c3d4e5f6g7h8", "CidrBlock": "10.0.1.0/24", "AvailabilityZone": "us-east-1a", "State": "available", "AvailableIpAddressCount": 251, "Tags": [{ "Key": "Name", "Value": "anihpj-public-web-1a" }] } } ✓ Subnet created Subnet ID: subnet-0a1b2c3d4e5f6g7h ✓ Auto-assign public IP enabled
# Create public subnet for web tier from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost from django.utils import timezone public_subnet = { 'subnet_id': 'subnet-0a1b2c3d4e5f6g7h', 'vpc_id': 'vpc-0a1b2c3d4e5f6g7h8', 'cidr': '10.0.1.0/24', 'availability_zone': 'us-east-1a', 'type': 'Public', 'layer': 'Web' } print(f"Public Subnet Created - Web Tier") print(f"Subnet ID: {public_subnet['subnet_id']}") print(f"CIDR Block: {public_subnet['cidr']}") print(f"AZ: {public_subnet['availability_zone']}") print(f"") # IP address allocation ip_allocation = { 'total_ips': 256, 'reserved_ips': 5, 'usable_ips': 251, 'allocated': 0, 'available': 251 } print(f"IP address allocation:") print(f" Total IPs: {ip_allocation['total_ips']}") print(f" Reserved by AWS: {ip_allocation['reserved_ips']}") print(f" Usable IPs: {ip_allocation['usable_ips']}") print(f" Available: {ip_allocation['available']}") print(f"") # Reserved IPs explanation reserved_ips = { '10.0.1.0': 'Network address', '10.0.1.1': 'VPC router', '10.0.1.2': 'DNS server', '10.0.1.3': 'Future use', '10.0.1.255': 'Broadcast address' } print(f"Reserved IP addresses:") for ip, purpose in reserved_ips.items(): print(f" {ip}: {purpose}") print(f"") # Web tier resources web_tier_resources = { 'load_balancers': 1, 'nat_gateways': 0, 'web_servers': 5, 'bastion_hosts': 1 } print(f"Web tier resources in this subnet:") for resource, count in web_tier_resources.items(): print(f" {resource}: {count}") # Network configuration network_config = { 'map_public_ip': True, 'auto_assign_public_ip': True, 'route_table': 'Public route table (0.0.0.0/0 → IGW)', 'nacl': 'Default (allow all)' } print(f"\nNetwork configuration:") for setting, value in network_config.items(): print(f" {setting}: {value}") # Instance types suitable for this subnet suitable_instances = { 'alb': 'Application Load Balancer', 'nat': 'NAT gateway or instance', 'bastion': 'Bastion/Jump host', 'web_server': 'Nginx, Apache, Django Gunicorn' } print(f"\nSuitable resources for public subnet:") for resource, purpose in suitable_instances.items(): print(f" - {purpose}") # Traffic flow traffic_flow = { 'inbound': 'Internet → ALB → Web servers', 'outbound': 'Web servers → Internet (via IGW)', 'internal': 'Web servers ↔ App tier (10.0.10.0/24)' } print(f"\nTraffic flow:") for direction, flow in traffic_flow.items(): print(f" {direction}: {flow}") print(f"\n✓ Public subnet created") print(f"✓ Web tier ready for ALB and web servers")
Example 2: Create Private Subnet for Application Tier

Create a private subnet for Django application servers that don't need direct internet access.

#!/bin/bash
# Create private subnet for application tier
VPC_ID="vpc-0a1b2c3d4e5f6g7h8"
SUBNET_CIDR="10.0.10.0/24"
AZ="us-east-1a"
REGION="us-east-1"
SUBNET_NAME="anihpj-private-app-1a"

echo "Creating private subnet for application tier..."
echo "VPC ID: $VPC_ID"
echo "CIDR: $SUBNET_CIDR"
echo "AZ: $AZ"
echo ""

# Create the private subnet
SUBNET_ID=$(aws ec2 create-subnet \
  --vpc-id $VPC_ID \
  --cidr-block $SUBNET_CIDR \
  --availability-zone $AZ \
  --tag-specifications "ResourceType=subnet,Tags=[
    {Key=Name,Value=$SUBNET_NAME},
    {Key=Type,Value=Private},
    {Key=Layer,Value=Application}
  ]" \
  --region $REGION \
  --query 'Subnet.SubnetId' \
  --output text)

echo "✓ Subnet created"
echo "Subnet ID: $SUBNET_ID"
echo ""

echo "Private subnet configuration:"
echo "  Type: Private (no direct internet)"
echo "  Layer: Application tier (Django servers)"
echo "  Access: Via NAT Gateway from public subnet"
echo "  Available IPs: 251 (minus 5 reserved)"
Creating private subnet for application tier... VPC ID: vpc-0a1b2c3d4e5f6g7h8 CIDR: 10.0.10.0/24 AZ: us-east-1a { "Subnet": { "SubnetId": "subnet-1b2c3d4e5f6g7h8i", "VpcId": "vpc-0a1b2c3d4e5f6g7h8", "CidrBlock": "10.0.10.0/24", "AvailabilityZone": "us-east-1a", "State": "available", "AvailableIpAddressCount": 251, "Tags": [{ "Key": "Name", "Value": "anihpj-private-app-1a" }] } } ✓ Subnet created Subnet ID: subnet-1b2c3d4e5f6g7h8i
# Create private subnet for application tier from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost from django.utils import timezone private_app_subnet = { 'subnet_id': 'subnet-1b2c3d4e5f6g7h8i', 'vpc_id': 'vpc-0a1b2c3d4e5f6g7h8', 'cidr': '10.0.10.0/24', 'availability_zone': 'us-east-1a', 'type': 'Private', 'layer': 'Application' } print(f"Private Subnet Created - Application Tier") print(f"Subnet ID: {private_app_subnet['subnet_id']}") print(f"CIDR Block: {private_app_subnet['cidr']}") print(f"AZ: {private_app_subnet['availability_zone']}") print(f"Type: {private_app_subnet['type']}") print(f"") # IP address usage ip_usage = { 'total_ips': 256, 'reserved_ips': 5, 'usable_ips': 251, 'django_instances': 3, 'future_capacity': 248 } print(f"IP address capacity:") print(f" Total IPs: {ip_usage['total_ips']}") print(f" Reserved: {ip_usage['reserved_ips']}") print(f" Usable: {ip_usage['usable_ips']}") print(f" Current usage: {ip_usage['django_instances']} Django instances") print(f"") # Django instances in this subnet django_instances = { 'i-app1': '10.0.10.50 (Gunicorn worker 1)', 'i-app2': '10.0.10.51 (Gunicorn worker 2)', 'i-app3': '10.0.10.52 (Gunicorn worker 3)' } print(f"Django application instances:") for instance_id, details in django_instances.items(): print(f" {instance_id}: {details}") print(f"") # Network connectivity connectivity = { 'internet_access': 'Via NAT Gateway (public subnet)', 'inbound_from': 'ALB (10.0.1.0/24)', 'outbound_to': 'Database (10.0.20.0/24), Cache (10.0.30.0/24)', 'vpn_access': 'Systems Manager Session Manager' } print(f"Network connectivity:") for access_type, route in connectivity.items(): print(f" {access_type}: {route}") # Security configuration security = { 'security_group': 'sg-app (allow from ALB on 8000)', 'nacl': 'Default (allow all)', 'public_ip': False, 'nat_required': True } print(f"\nSecurity configuration:") for setting, value in security.items(): print(f" {setting}: {value}") # Django application details app_stack = { 'framework': 'Django 5.2.5', 'workers': 3, 'threads_per_worker': 4, 'total_capacity': '12 concurrent requests' } print(f"\nDjango application stack:") for component, value in app_stack.items(): print(f" {component}: {value}") # Outbound route configuration outbound_routes = { 'destination_database': '10.0.20.0/24 (Direct)', 'destination_cache': '10.0.30.0/24 (Direct)', 'destination_internet': '0.0.0.0/0 (via NAT Gateway)' } print(f"\nOutbound routes:") for destination, route in outbound_routes.items(): print(f" {destination}: {route}") print(f"\n✓ Private application subnet created") print(f"✓ Django instances can be deployed")
Example 3: Create Database Subnet in Different AZ (Multi-AZ HA)

Create a database subnet in a different availability zone to enable multi-AZ RDS deployment for high availability.

#!/bin/bash
# Create private database subnet in second AZ
VPC_ID="vpc-0a1b2c3d4e5f6g7h8"
SUBNET_CIDR="10.0.20.0/24"
AZ="us-east-1b"
REGION="us-east-1"
SUBNET_NAME="anihpj-private-db-1b"

echo "Creating database subnet for multi-AZ HA..."
echo "VPC ID: $VPC_ID"
echo "CIDR: $SUBNET_CIDR"
echo "AZ: $AZ (different from app tier)"
echo ""

# Create the database subnet in AZ-1b
SUBNET_ID=$(aws ec2 create-subnet \
  --vpc-id $VPC_ID \
  --cidr-block $SUBNET_CIDR \
  --availability-zone $AZ \
  --tag-specifications "ResourceType=subnet,Tags=[
    {Key=Name,Value=$SUBNET_NAME},
    {Key=Type,Value=Private},
    {Key=Layer,Value=Database},
    {Key=DBSubnetGroup,Value=anihpj-db-subnet-group}
  ]" \
  --region $REGION \
  --query 'Subnet.SubnetId' \
  --output text)

echo "✓ Subnet created"
echo "Subnet ID: $SUBNET_ID"
echo ""

echo "Multi-AZ RDS Configuration:"
echo "  Primary DB (AZ-1a): subnet-db-1a (10.0.20.0/24)"
echo "  Standby DB (AZ-1b): $SUBNET_ID (10.0.20.0/24) ← NEW"
echo "  Failover: Automatic (< 1 minute)"
echo "  Data replication: Synchronous"
Creating database subnet for multi-AZ HA... VPC ID: vpc-0a1b2c3d4e5f6g7h8 CIDR: 10.0.20.0/24 AZ: us-east-1b { "Subnet": { "SubnetId": "subnet-2c3d4e5f6g7h8i9j", "VpcId": "vpc-0a1b2c3d4e5f6g7h8", "CidrBlock": "10.0.20.0/24", "AvailabilityZone": "us-east-1b", "State": "available", "AvailableIpAddressCount": 251, "Tags": [{ "Key": "Name", "Value": "anihpj-private-db-1b" }] } } ✓ Subnet created Subnet ID: subnet-2c3d4e5f6g7h8i9j
# Create database subnet for multi-AZ from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost from django.utils import timezone db_subnet_multi_az = { 'subnet_id': 'subnet-2c3d4e5f6g7h8i9j', 'vpc_id': 'vpc-0a1b2c3d4e5f6g7h8', 'cidr': '10.0.20.0/24', 'availability_zone': 'us-east-1b', 'purpose': 'Multi-AZ RDS standby' } print(f"Database Subnet Created - Multi-AZ HA") print(f"Subnet ID: {db_subnet_multi_az['subnet_id']}") print(f"CIDR Block: {db_subnet_multi_az['cidr']}") print(f"AZ: {db_subnet_multi_az['availability_zone']}") print(f"") # Multi-AZ deployment configuration multi_az_config = { 'primary_az': 'us-east-1a', 'primary_subnet': 'subnet-db-1a', 'standby_az': 'us-east-1b', 'standby_subnet': 'subnet-2c3d4e5f6g7h8i9j', 'failover_time': '< 60 seconds', 'data_loss': 'Zero (synchronous replication)' } print(f"Multi-AZ RDS Configuration:") for setting, value in multi_az_config.items(): print(f" {setting}: {value}") print(f"") # RDS instance details rds_instances = { 'primary': { 'instance_id': 'anihpj-db-primary', 'instance_class': 'db.r6i.xlarge', 'engine': 'MySQL 8.0', 'storage_gb': 500, 'az': 'us-east-1a' }, 'standby': { 'instance_id': 'anihpj-db-primary-standby', 'instance_class': 'db.r6i.xlarge', 'engine': 'MySQL 8.0', 'storage_gb': 500, 'az': 'us-east-1b' } } print(f"RDS multi-AZ deployment:") for role, details in rds_instances.items(): print(f" {role.upper()}:") print(f" Instance: {details['instance_id']}") print(f" Type: {details['instance_class']}") print(f" Storage: {details['storage_gb']}GB") print(f" AZ: {details['az']}") print(f"") # Replication configuration replication = { 'replication_type': 'Synchronous (Multi-AZ)', 'replication_lag': '0ms (same region)', 'bandwidth': '10 Gbps (VPC internal)', 'data_consistency': 'Strong (ACID compliant)' } print(f"Replication configuration:") for setting, value in replication.items(): print(f" {setting}: {value}") # Failover behavior failover = { 'trigger': 'Primary AZ outage or manual failover', 'detection': 'Automatic (30 seconds)', 'failover_time': '< 1 minute', 'dns_update': 'Automatic (endpoint unchanged)', 'application_impact': 'Brief connection reset, auto-reconnect' } print(f"\nFailover behavior:") for setting, value in failover.items(): print(f" {setting}: {value}") # Database connections db_connections = { 'application_servers': 'All AZs', 'connection_pool': 100, 'timeout_seconds': 30, 'max_connections': 'db.r6i.xlarge: 1000' } print(f"\nDatabase connections:") for setting, value in db_connections.items(): print(f" {setting}: {value}") # RTO and RPO metrics disaster_recovery = { 'rto_recovery_time': '< 1 minute', 'rpo_data_loss': '0 transactions', 'availability': '99.95% SLA', 'backup_retention': '30 days' } print(f"\nDisaster Recovery metrics:") for metric, value in disaster_recovery.items(): print(f" {metric}: {value}") print(f"\n✓ Multi-AZ database subnet created") print(f"✓ High availability database ready")
Example 4: Create Multiple Subnets in Bulk Using Variables

Create multiple subnets at once for a complete multi-tier, multi-AZ infrastructure.

#!/bin/bash
# Create multiple subnets for complete infrastructure
VPC_ID="vpc-0a1b2c3d4e5f6g7h8"
REGION="us-east-1"

# Define subnets: NAME, CIDR, AZ
declare -A subnets=(
  ["public-web-1a"]="10.0.1.0/24:us-east-1a:Public"
  ["public-web-1b"]="10.0.2.0/24:us-east-1b:Public"
  ["private-app-1a"]="10.0.10.0/24:us-east-1a:Private"
  ["private-app-1b"]="10.0.11.0/24:us-east-1b:Private"
  ["private-db-1a"]="10.0.20.0/24:us-east-1a:Private"
  ["private-db-1b"]="10.0.21.0/24:us-east-1b:Private"
)

echo "Creating multi-tier, multi-AZ subnet architecture..."
echo ""

declare -A subnet_ids

for subnet_name in "${!subnets[@]}"; do
  IFS=':' read -r cidr az subnet_type <<< "${subnets[$subnet_name]}"
  
  echo "Creating $subnet_name ($cidr in $az)"
  
  subnet_id=$(aws ec2 create-subnet \
    --vpc-id $VPC_ID \
    --cidr-block $cidr \
    --availability-zone $az \
    --tag-specifications "ResourceType=subnet,Tags=[
      {Key=Name,Value=anihpj-$subnet_name},
      {Key=Type,Value=$subnet_type},
      {Key=Layer,Value=${subnet_name#*-}}
    ]" \
    --region $REGION \
    --query 'Subnet.SubnetId' \
    --output text)
  
  subnet_ids[$subnet_name]=$subnet_id
  echo "  ✓ Created: $subnet_id"
done

echo ""
echo "Subnet architecture created:"
echo "  Public (Web): 2 subnets across 2 AZs"
echo "  Private (App): 2 subnets across 2 AZs"
echo "  Private (DB): 2 subnets across 2 AZs"
echo "  Total: 6 subnets (18 IP ranges available)"
Creating multi-tier, multi-AZ subnet architecture... Creating public-web-1a (10.0.1.0/24 in us-east-1a) ✓ Created: subnet-0a1b2c3d4e5f6g7h Creating public-web-1b (10.0.2.0/24 in us-east-1b) ✓ Created: subnet-0b2c3d4e5f6g7h8i Creating private-app-1a (10.0.10.0/24 in us-east-1a) ✓ Created: subnet-1b2c3d4e5f6g7h8i Creating private-app-1b (10.0.11.0/24 in us-east-1b) ✓ Created: subnet-1c3d4e5f6g7h8i9j Creating private-db-1a (10.0.20.0/24 in us-east-1a) ✓ Created: subnet-2c3d4e5f6g7h8i9j Creating private-db-1b (10.0.21.0/24 in us-east-1b) ✓ Created: subnet-2d4e5f6g7h8i9j0k Subnet architecture created: Public (Web): 2 subnets across 2 AZs Private (App): 2 subnets across 2 AZs Private (DB): 2 subnets across 2 AZs Total: 6 subnets (18 IP ranges available)
# Create multiple subnets for complete infrastructure from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost from django.utils import timezone import json # Multi-tier, multi-AZ subnet architecture subnets_created = { 'public_web': [ { 'name': 'public-web-1a', 'subnet_id': 'subnet-0a1b2c3d4e5f6g7h', 'cidr': '10.0.1.0/24', 'az': 'us-east-1a', 'type': 'Public', 'tier': 'Web' }, { 'name': 'public-web-1b', 'subnet_id': 'subnet-0b2c3d4e5f6g7h8i', 'cidr': '10.0.2.0/24', 'az': 'us-east-1b', 'type': 'Public', 'tier': 'Web' } ], 'private_app': [ { 'name': 'private-app-1a', 'subnet_id': 'subnet-1b2c3d4e5f6g7h8i', 'cidr': '10.0.10.0/24', 'az': 'us-east-1a', 'type': 'Private', 'tier': 'Application' }, { 'name': 'private-app-1b', 'subnet_id': 'subnet-1c3d4e5f6g7h8i9j', 'cidr': '10.0.11.0/24', 'az': 'us-east-1b', 'type': 'Private', 'tier': 'Application' } ], 'private_db': [ { 'name': 'private-db-1a', 'subnet_id': 'subnet-2c3d4e5f6g7h8i9j', 'cidr': '10.0.20.0/24', 'az': 'us-east-1a', 'type': 'Private', 'tier': 'Database' }, { 'name': 'private-db-1b', 'subnet_id': 'subnet-2d4e5f6g7h8i9j0k', 'cidr': '10.0.21.0/24', 'az': 'us-east-1b', 'type': 'Private', 'tier': 'Database' } ] } print(f"Multi-Tier, Multi-AZ Infrastructure Created") print(f"=" * 60) print(f"") # Summary statistics total_subnets = len(subnets_created['public_web']) + len(subnets_created['private_app']) + len(subnets_created['private_db']) total_ips = total_subnets * 256 # 256 IPs per /24 usable_ips = total_subnets * 251 # minus 5 reserved per subnet print(f"Summary:") print(f" Total subnets: {total_subnets}") print(f" Total IP addresses: {total_ips}") print(f" Usable IP addresses: {usable_ips}") print(f"") # Public web tier print(f"Public Web Tier (2 AZs):") for subnet in subnets_created['public_web']: print(f" {subnet['name']}:") print(f" Subnet ID: {subnet['subnet_id']}") print(f" CIDR: {subnet['cidr']}") print(f" AZ: {subnet['az']}") print(f"") # Private application tier print(f"Private Application Tier (2 AZs):") for subnet in subnets_created['private_app']: print(f" {subnet['name']}:") print(f" Subnet ID: {subnet['subnet_id']}") print(f" CIDR: {subnet['cidr']}") print(f" AZ: {subnet['az']}") print(f"") # Private database tier print(f"Private Database Tier (2 AZs):") for subnet in subnets_created['private_db']: print(f" {subnet['name']}:") print(f" Subnet ID: {subnet['subnet_id']}") print(f" CIDR: {subnet['cidr']}") print(f" AZ: {subnet['az']}") print(f"") # High availability configuration ha_config = { 'multi_az': True, 'active_active': 'Public tier (ALB)', 'active_passive': 'App and DB tiers', 'failover_capability': 'Automatic (ALB health checks)', 'rto_seconds': 60, 'rpo_transactions': 0 } print(f"High Availability Configuration:") for setting, value in ha_config.items(): print(f" {setting}: {value}") print(f"") # Traffic flow between tiers traffic_patterns = { 'client_to_alb': 'Internet → ALB (public subnet)', 'alb_to_app': 'Public (10.0.1-2.0/24) → App (10.0.10-11.0/24)', 'app_to_db': 'App (10.0.10-11.0/24) → DB (10.0.20-21.0/24)', 'app_to_internet': 'App → NAT → IGW (via public subnet)', 'db_replication': 'DB (1a) ↔ DB (1b) synchronous' } print(f"Traffic Flow Patterns:") for pattern, flow in traffic_patterns.items(): print(f" {pattern}: {flow}") print(f"") # Resource distribution resource_distribution = { 'public_web_tier': { 'alb': 2, 'nat_gateways': 2, 'bastion_hosts': 1, 'available_ips': 502 }, 'private_app_tier': { 'django_servers': 6, 'celery_workers': 3, 'redis_replica': 1, 'available_ips': 502 }, 'private_db_tier': { 'rds_primary': 1, 'rds_standby': 1, 'available_ips': 502 } } print(f"Resource Distribution:") for tier, resources in resource_distribution.items(): tier_name = tier.replace('_', ' ').title() print(f" {tier_name}:") for resource, count in resources.items(): print(f" {resource}: {count}") print(f"") # Cost optimization cost_optimization = { 'nat_gateway_placement': 'One per AZ (high availability)', 'elastic_ip_allocation': 'One per NAT Gateway', 'public_subnet_ips': 'Only for ALB and NAT', 'private_subnet_ips': 'Primary for instances', 'unused_ip_savings': 'Reserve for future scaling' } print(f"Cost Optimization:") for strategy, description in cost_optimization.items(): print(f" {strategy}: {description}") # Security zones security_zones = { 'zone_1_public': 'DMZ (public web tier)', 'zone_2_private': 'Application tier (private)', 'zone_3_private': 'Database tier (private, restricted)' } print(f"\nSecurity Zones:") for zone, purpose in security_zones.items(): print(f" {zone}: {purpose}") print(f"\n✓ Complete 3-tier, multi-AZ infrastructure created") print(f"✓ Ready for production deployment")
Example 5: Create Cache Layer Subnet with Custom DHCP Options

Create a dedicated subnet for ElastiCache (Redis/Memcached) with custom DNS and DHCP options for monitoring.

#!/bin/bash
# Create cache tier subnet with custom DHCP
VPC_ID="vpc-0a1b2c3d4e5f6g7h8"
SUBNET_CIDR="10.0.30.0/24"
AZ="us-east-1a"
REGION="us-east-1"
SUBNET_NAME="anihpj-private-cache-1a"

echo "Creating ElastiCache subnet with monitoring..."
echo ""

# Create DHCP options set for monitoring
DHCP_OPTIONS=$(aws ec2 create-dhcp-options \
  --dhcp-configuration \
    Key=domain-name,Values=ec2.internal \
    Key=domain-name-servers,Values=AmazonProvidedDNS \
    Key=ntp-servers,Values=169.254.169.123 \
  --tag-specifications "ResourceType=dhcp-options,Tags=[
    {Key=Name,Value=anihpj-cache-monitoring}
  ]" \
  --region $REGION \
  --query 'DhcpOptions.DhcpOptionsId' \
  --output text)

echo "✓ DHCP options created: $DHCP_OPTIONS"
echo ""

# Create the cache subnet
SUBNET_ID=$(aws ec2 create-subnet \
  --vpc-id $VPC_ID \
  --cidr-block $SUBNET_CIDR \
  --availability-zone $AZ \
  --tag-specifications "ResourceType=subnet,Tags=[
    {Key=Name,Value=$SUBNET_NAME},
    {Key=Type,Value=Private},
    {Key=Layer,Value=Cache},
    {Key=ServiceType,Value=ElastiCache}
  ]" \
  --region $REGION \
  --query 'Subnet.SubnetId' \
  --output text)

echo "✓ Subnet created"
echo "Subnet ID: $SUBNET_ID"
echo ""

# Enable DNS resolution
aws ec2 modify-subnet-attribute \
  --subnet-id $SUBNET_ID \
  --enable-dns64 \
  --region $REGION

echo "✓ DNS64 enabled for IPv6 support"
echo ""

echo "ElastiCache Configuration:"
echo "  Instance: cache.r6g.xlarge (ARM-based, 26GB)"
echo "  Engine: Redis 7.0"
echo "  Mode: Cluster mode enabled"
echo "  Nodes: 3 (shards)"
echo "  Replicas: 2 per shard"
echo "  Automatic failover: Enabled"
echo "  Encryption: In-transit and at-rest"
echo "  Subnet group: anihpj-cache-subnet-group"
Creating ElastiCache subnet with monitoring... ✓ DHCP options created: dopt-0123456789abcdef0 { "Subnet": { "SubnetId": "subnet-3d4e5f6g7h8i9j0k1", "VpcId": "vpc-0a1b2c3d4e5f6g7h8", "CidrBlock": "10.0.30.0/24", "AvailabilityZone": "us-east-1a", "State": "available", "AvailableIpAddressCount": 251, "Tags": [{ "Key": "Name", "Value": "anihpj-private-cache-1a" }] } } ✓ Subnet created Subnet ID: subnet-3d4e5f6g7h8i9j0k1 ✓ DNS64 enabled for IPv6 support ElastiCache Configuration: Instance: cache.r6g.xlarge (26GB) Engine: Redis 7.0 Mode: Cluster mode enabled Nodes: 3 (shards) Replicas: 2 per shard Automatic failover: Enabled Encryption: In-transit and at-rest Subnet group: anihpj-cache-subnet-group
# Create cache tier subnet with ElastiCache configuration from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost from django.utils import timezone from django.core.cache import cache cache_subnet = { 'subnet_id': 'subnet-3d4e5f6g7h8i9j0k1', 'vpc_id': 'vpc-0a1b2c3d4e5f6g7h8', 'cidr': '10.0.30.0/24', 'availability_zone': 'us-east-1a', 'service': 'ElastiCache Redis', 'purpose': 'Distributed caching layer' } print(f"Cache Layer Subnet Created") print(f"Subnet ID: {cache_subnet['subnet_id']}") print(f"CIDR Block: {cache_subnet['cidr']}") print(f"AZ: {cache_subnet['availability_zone']}") print(f"Service: {cache_subnet['service']}") print(f"") # ElastiCache Redis cluster configuration redis_cluster = { 'cluster_name': 'anihpj-cache-prod', 'engine': 'Redis 7.0', 'node_type': 'cache.r6g.xlarge', 'memory_gb': 26, 'network_bandwidth_gbps': 20, 'num_cache_nodes': 3, 'num_replicas': 2, 'total_nodes': 9, 'auto_failover': True, 'multi_az': True } print(f"Redis Cluster Configuration:") for setting, value in redis_cluster.items(): if isinstance(value, bool): value = 'Enabled' if value else 'Disabled' print(f" {setting}: {value}") print(f"") # Capacity and performance capacity_metrics = { 'total_memory': '26GB × 3 shards = 78GB', 'network_throughput': '20 Gbps per node', 'concurrent_connections': '65,000 per node', 'ops_per_second': '1,000,000+ combined', 'latency_p50': '< 1ms', 'latency_p99': '< 10ms' } print(f"Capacity & Performance:") for metric, value in capacity_metrics.items(): print(f" {metric}: {value}") print(f"") # Cache usage patterns for Django django_cache_patterns = { 'session_storage': 'Redis (session expiry: 3600s)', 'query_result_cache': 'JobPost queries, Author profiles', 'celery_broker': 'Task queues, retry management', 'rate_limiting': 'API rate limits per user', 'real_time_data': 'Live job counts, trending skills' } print(f"Django Cache Patterns:") for pattern, usage in django_cache_patterns.items(): print(f" {pattern}:") print(f" {usage}") print(f"") # Specific cacheable Django models cache_strategy = { 'job_posts': { 'ttl_seconds': 300, 'key': 'jobpost:list:published', 'size': '1-5MB per region', 'refresh': 'On publish/update' }, 'user_profiles': { 'ttl_seconds': 1800, 'key': 'user:profile:{user_id}', 'size': '100KB per user', 'refresh': 'On login' }, 'skills_taxonomy': { 'ttl_seconds': 3600, 'key': 'skills:all', 'size': '500KB', 'refresh': 'Hourly' }, 'location_data': { 'ttl_seconds': 7200, 'key': 'locations:geo:{city}', 'size': '2MB', 'refresh': '2x daily' } } print(f"Cache Strategy by Model:") for model, strategy in cache_strategy.items(): print(f" {model}:") for setting, value in strategy.items(): print(f" {setting}: {value}") print(f"") # Connection pooling from application tier connection_config = { 'primary_endpoint': 'anihpj-cache.xxxxx.ng.0001.use1.cache.amazonaws.com:6379', 'reader_endpoint': 'anihpj-cache-ro.xxxxx.ng.0001.use1.cache.amazonaws.com:6379', 'connections_per_app': 50, 'max_app_servers': 6, 'total_connections': '300 max', 'pool_timeout': 30 } print(f"Connection Configuration:") for setting, value in connection_config.items(): print(f" {setting}: {value}") # Monitoring and alerts monitoring = { 'cloudwatch_metrics': [ 'CPU Utilization', 'Network Bytes In/Out', 'Evictions (memory pressure)', 'Replication Lag', 'Failed Commands' ], 'alarm_triggers': { 'cpu_high': '> 75%', 'evictions_rate': '> 100/sec', 'replication_lag': '> 1000ms', 'memory_limit': '> 90% utilized' } } print(f"\nMonitoring & Alerts:") print(f" CloudWatch Metrics: {', '.join(monitoring['cloudwatch_metrics'][:3])}") for trigger, threshold in monitoring['alarm_triggers'].items(): print(f" Alert - {trigger}: {threshold}") # Security configuration security = { 'subnet': cache_subnet['subnet_id'], 'security_group': 'sg-cache', 'ingress_ports': '6379 (Redis)', 'allowed_from': 'App tier security group (sg-app)', 'encryption_transit': 'TLS 1.2+', 'encryption_rest': 'AES-256', 'auth_token': 'Enabled (IAM or Redis AUTH)' } print(f"\nSecurity Configuration:") for setting, value in security.items(): print(f" {setting}: {value}") print(f"\n✓ ElastiCache Redis subnet created") print(f"✓ Cache layer ready for Django deployment")

create-internet-gateway

Create an internet gateway (IGW) for a VPC. An internet gateway serves as the bridge between your VPC and the internet, enabling resources in public subnets to communicate with the internet and vice versa. Each VPC can have only one IGW, but the IGW can be attached or detached from the VPC as needed.

Syntax
aws ec2 create-internet-gateway \
  [--tag-specifications 'ResourceType=internet-gateway,Tags=[{Key=Name,Value=}]'] \
  [--region ]
Parameters Description
  • --tag-specifications (string): Tags to apply to the internet gateway. Format: ResourceType=internet-gateway,Tags=[{Key=Name,Value=my-igw}]
  • --region (string): AWS region where the internet gateway will be created (us-east-1, eu-west-1, etc.)
  • --dry-run (boolean): Check if the operation would succeed without actually creating the resource
Return Value
  • InternetGateway (Object): The created internet gateway with detailed information
  • InternetGatewayId (String): Unique IGW identifier (igw-xxxxxxxx)
  • Attachments (Array): List of VPCs the IGW is attached to (empty initially)
  • State (String): Current attachment state (attached, available)
  • Tags (Array): Tags applied to the internet gateway
  • OwnerId (String): AWS account ID that owns the IGW
Example 1: Create Production Internet Gateway with Naming Tags

Create an internet gateway for the production VPC with clear naming and cost allocation tags.

#!/bin/bash
# Create production internet gateway
VPC_ID="vpc-0a1b2c3d4e5f6g7h8"
REGION="us-east-1"
IGW_NAME="anihpj-prod-igw"

echo "Creating internet gateway for production VPC..."
echo "VPC ID: $VPC_ID"
echo ""

# Create the internet gateway
IGW_ID=$(aws ec2 create-internet-gateway \
  --tag-specifications "ResourceType=internet-gateway,Tags=[
    {Key=Name,Value=$IGW_NAME},
    {Key=Environment,Value=Production},
    {Key=ServiceType,Value=Internet-Gateway},
    {Key=CostCenter,Value=Engineering}
  ]" \
  --region $REGION \
  --query 'InternetGateway.InternetGatewayId' \
  --output text)

echo "✓ Internet gateway created"
echo "IGW ID: $IGW_ID"
echo ""

# Attach to production VPC
echo "Attaching internet gateway to VPC..."
aws ec2 attach-internet-gateway \
  --internet-gateway-id $IGW_ID \
  --vpc-id $VPC_ID \
  --region $REGION

echo "✓ IGW attached to VPC"
echo ""

echo "Internet Gateway Configuration:"
echo "  Purpose: Enable public subnet internet access"
echo "  Attachment: VPC $VPC_ID"
echo "  State: attached"
echo "  High Availability: Inherent (redundant across AZs)"
Creating internet gateway for production VPC... VPC ID: vpc-0a1b2c3d4e5f6g7h8 { "InternetGateway": { "InternetGatewayId": "igw-0a1b2c3d4e5f6g7h", "Attachments": [], "Tags": [{ "Key": "Name", "Value": "anihpj-prod-igw" }], "OwnerId": "123456789012" } } ✓ Internet gateway created IGW ID: igw-0a1b2c3d4e5f6g7h ✓ IGW attached to VPC Internet Gateway Configuration: Purpose: Enable public subnet internet access Attachment: VPC vpc-0a1b2c3d4e5f6g7h8 State: attached High Availability: Inherent (redundant across AZs)
# Create production internet gateway from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost from django.utils import timezone production_igw = { 'igw_id': 'igw-0a1b2c3d4e5f6g7h', 'vpc_id': 'vpc-0a1b2c3d4e5f6g7h8', 'name': 'anihpj-prod-igw', 'environment': 'Production', 'state': 'attached' } print(f"Production Internet Gateway Created") print(f"IGW ID: {production_igw['igw_id']}") print(f"VPC ID: {production_igw['vpc_id']}") print(f"Environment: {production_igw['environment']}") print(f"State: {production_igw['state']}") print(f"") # IGW connectivity flow connectivity_flow = { 'tier_1_public': { 'name': 'Public Web Tier', 'subnets': ['subnet-0a1b2c3d4e5f6g7h (10.0.1.0/24)', 'subnet-0b2c3d4e5f6g7h8i (10.0.2.0/24)'], 'route_target': 'IGW (0.0.0.0/0)', 'traffic': 'Client ↔ ALB ↔ Internet' }, 'tier_2_private_app': { 'name': 'Private Application Tier', 'subnets': ['subnet-1b2c3d4e5f6g7h8i (10.0.10.0/24)', 'subnet-1c3d4e5f6g7h8i9j (10.0.11.0/24)'], 'route_target': 'NAT Gateway (in public subnet)', 'traffic': 'App → NAT → IGW → Internet' }, 'tier_3_private_db': { 'name': 'Private Database Tier', 'subnets': ['subnet-2c3d4e5f6g7h8i9j (10.0.20.0/24)', 'subnet-2d4e5f6g7h8i9j0k (10.0.21.0/24)'], 'route_target': 'None (no internet)', 'traffic': 'DB ↔ App tier (internal only)' } } print(f"VPC Traffic Routing:") for tier, config in connectivity_flow.items(): print(f" {config['name']}:") print(f" Route: {config['route_target']}") print(f" Traffic: {config['traffic']}") print(f"") # Public subnet internet routing public_routes = { 'route_1': '10.0.0.0/16 → local (internal VPC)', 'route_2': '0.0.0.0/0 → igw-0a1b2c3d4e5f6g7h (internet)', } print(f"Public Subnet Route Table:") for route, target in public_routes.items(): print(f" {route}: {target}") print(f"") # Inbound internet traffic to ALB inbound_traffic = { 'source': 'Internet (0.0.0.0/0)', 'destination': 'ALB (10.0.1.0/24, 10.0.2.0/24)', 'ports': ['80 (HTTP)', '443 (HTTPS)'], 'vpc_flow': 'IGW → Public subnet → ALB → App tier' } print(f"Inbound Internet Traffic (HTTP/HTTPS):") for setting, value in inbound_traffic.items(): print(f" {setting}: {value}") # Outbound internet traffic from app tier outbound_traffic = { 'source': 'Django app servers (10.0.10.0/24, 10.0.11.0/24)', 'destination': 'External APIs/Services (e.g., payment, email)', 'route': 'App → NAT → IGW → Internet', 'nat_gateway': 'Elastic IP in public subnet' } print(f"\nOutbound Internet Traffic (from App tier):") for setting, value in outbound_traffic.items(): print(f" {setting}: {value}") # HA and resilience high_availability = { 'igw_redundancy': 'Inherent (AWS manages across AZs)', 'single_point_of_failure': 'IGW attachment (mitigated by design)', 'nat_gateway_redundancy': 'One per AZ required for HA', 'failover_mechanism': 'Multi-AZ NAT Gateways in public subnets', 'rto_seconds': '0 (no failover needed)', 'automatic_recovery': True } print(f"\nHigh Availability & Resilience:") for setting, value in high_availability.items(): if isinstance(value, bool): value = 'Enabled' if value else 'Disabled' print(f" {setting}: {value}") # Associated NAT Gateways nat_gateways = { 'nat_gw_1': { 'id': 'nat-0a1b2c3d4e5f6g7h', 'eip': '203.0.113.10', 'subnet': 'subnet-0a1b2c3d4e5f6g7h (us-east-1a)', 'serves': 'App tier in us-east-1a' }, 'nat_gw_2': { 'id': 'nat-1b2c3d4e5f6g7h8i', 'eip': '203.0.113.11', 'subnet': 'subnet-0b2c3d4e5f6g7h8i (us-east-1b)', 'serves': 'App tier in us-east-1b' } } print(f"\nNAT Gateways (for App tier internet access):") for nat, config in nat_gateways.items(): print(f" {nat}:") print(f" Elastic IP: {config['eip']}") print(f" Location: {config['subnet']}") print(f" Serves: {config['serves']}") # Django application impact app_impact = { 'user_requests': 'Direct from internet → ALB → Django (10ms latency)', 'api_calls': 'Django → External APIs via NAT → Internet', 'database_access': 'Internal (no internet needed)', 'cdn_access': 'CloudFront (optional, separate distribution)', 'external_services': [ 'Email delivery (SendGrid)', 'Payment processing (Stripe)', 'Analytics (segment)', 'Error tracking (Sentry)' ] } print(f"\nDjango Application Impact:") print(f" User requests: {app_impact['user_requests']}") print(f" External services:") for service in app_impact['external_services']: print(f" - {service}") # Cost implications cost_info = { 'igw_hourly_cost': 0, 'igw_data_transfer': 'Free in/out over IGW', 'nat_gateway_hourly': '$0.045 per NAT GW per hour', 'nat_gateway_data_transfer': '$0.045 per GB processed', 'estimated_monthly_nat': '$32.40 + data transfer' } print(f"\nCost Implications:") for item, cost in cost_info.items(): print(f" {item}: {cost}") print(f"\n✓ Production IGW created") print(f"✓ Public subnet now has internet access")
Example 2: Create Staging Internet Gateway with Minimal Tags

Create an internet gateway for staging environment with minimal tagging for cost control.

#!/bin/bash
# Create staging internet gateway
VPC_ID="vpc-1b2c3d4e5f6g7h8i9"
REGION="us-east-1"
STAGING_IGW_NAME="anihpj-staging-igw"

echo "Creating staging environment internet gateway..."
echo ""

# Create IGW with minimal tags
IGW_ID=$(aws ec2 create-internet-gateway \
  --tag-specifications "ResourceType=internet-gateway,Tags=[
    {Key=Name,Value=$STAGING_IGW_NAME},
    {Key=Environment,Value=Staging}
  ]" \
  --region $REGION \
  --query 'InternetGateway.InternetGatewayId' \
  --output text)

echo "✓ Staging IGW created: $IGW_ID"
echo ""

# Attach to staging VPC
aws ec2 attach-internet-gateway \
  --internet-gateway-id $IGW_ID \
  --vpc-id $VPC_ID \
  --region $REGION

echo "✓ Attached to staging VPC: $VPC_ID"
echo ""

echo "Staging IGW ready for:"
echo "  - QA testing"
echo "  - Pre-production validation"
echo "  - Load testing"
echo "  - Django upgrade testing"
Creating staging environment internet gateway... { "InternetGateway": { "InternetGatewayId": "igw-1b2c3d4e5f6g7h8i", "Attachments": [], "Tags": [{ "Key": "Name", "Value": "anihpj-staging-igw" }], "OwnerId": "123456789012" } } ✓ Staging IGW created: igw-1b2c3d4e5f6g7h8i ✓ Attached to staging VPC: vpc-1b2c3d4e5f6g7h8i9
# Create staging internet gateway from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost from django.utils import timezone staging_igw = { 'igw_id': 'igw-1b2c3d4e5f6g7h8i', 'vpc_id': 'vpc-1b2c3d4e5f6g7h8i9', 'environment': 'Staging' } print(f"Staging Internet Gateway Created") print(f"IGW ID: {staging_igw['igw_id']}") print(f"VPC ID: {staging_igw['vpc_id']}") print(f"Environment: Staging/QA") print(f"") # Staging infrastructure scale staging_scale = { 'subnets': 4, 'instances_web': 2, 'instances_app': 2, 'instances_db': 1, 'total_instances': 5 } print(f"Staging Infrastructure Scale:") for resource, count in staging_scale.items(): print(f" {resource}: {count}") print(f"") # Staging testing scenarios testing_scenarios = { 'qa_testing': 'Functional testing against staging APIs', 'load_testing': 'Simulate production load (10% scale)', 'security_testing': 'Vulnerability scanning, penetration testing', 'upgrade_testing': 'Django version upgrades, dependency updates', 'integration_testing': 'Third-party API integration validation' } print(f"Testing Scenarios:") for scenario, description in testing_scenarios.items(): print(f" {scenario}: {description}") print(f"") # Staging vs Production comparison comparison = { 'instance_sizes': 'Smaller (t3.small vs t3.large)', 'databases': '1 RDS instance (no multi-AZ)', 'load_balancer': '1 ALB (vs 2 for HA)', 'cost_ratio': '~20% of production', 'auto_scaling': 'Disabled (fixed capacity)', 'monitoring': 'Basic CloudWatch metrics' } print(f"Staging vs Production:") for aspect, detail in comparison.items(): print(f" {aspect}: {detail}") # Staging testing workflow test_workflow = { 'developer_push': 'Code pushed to staging branch', 'ci_pipeline': 'Build and deploy to staging (15 min)', 'dns_route': 'test.anihpj.example.com → ALB', 'test_execution': 'Automated and manual tests (30 min)', 'approval': 'QA/tech lead approval required', 'production_deploy': 'Deploy to production (next step)' } print(f"\nStaging Testing Workflow:") for step, description in test_workflow.items(): print(f" {step}: {description}") # External API testing external_api_testing = { 'payment_provider': 'Stripe test mode', 'email_service': 'SendGrid sandbox', 'cdn': 'CloudFront test distribution', 'authentication': 'AWS IAM test policies' } print(f"\nExternal API Testing:") for service, mode in external_api_testing.items(): print(f" {service}: {mode}") # Django settings for staging django_settings = { 'debug_mode': 'False (like production)', 'allowed_hosts': 'test.anihpj.example.com', 'database': 'staging_db (MySQL)', 'cache': 'Redis (shared with prod)', 'static_files': 'S3 (same CDN prefix)', 'logging': 'Full logging enabled' } print(f"\nDjango Staging Configuration:") for setting, value in django_settings.items(): print(f" {setting}: {value}") print(f"\n✓ Staging IGW configured for QA testing")
Example 3: Create Development IGW Without Attachment (for later use)

Create an internet gateway for development but don't attach immediately, allowing for flexible VPC switching during development cycles.

#!/bin/bash
# Create development IGW (unattached)
REGION="us-east-1"
DEV_IGW_NAME="anihpj-dev-igw"

echo "Creating development internet gateway (unattached)..."
echo ""

# Create IGW without immediate attachment
IGW_ID=$(aws ec2 create-internet-gateway \
  --tag-specifications "ResourceType=internet-gateway,Tags=[
    {Key=Name,Value=$DEV_IGW_NAME},
    {Key=Environment,Value=Development},
    {Key=Purpose,Value=Temporary-Testing}
  ]" \
  --region $REGION \
  --query 'InternetGateway.InternetGatewayId' \
  --output text)

echo "✓ Development IGW created: $IGW_ID"
echo "✓ Status: Unattached (ready for VPC assignment)"
echo ""

# Describe the created IGW
aws ec2 describe-internet-gateways \
  --internet-gateway-ids $IGW_ID \
  --region $REGION \
  --query 'InternetGateways[0].[InternetGatewayId,Tags[?Key==`Name`].Value|[0]]' \
  --output text

echo ""
echo "Next step: Attach to development VPC when needed"
echo "Command: aws ec2 attach-internet-gateway --internet-gateway-id $IGW_ID --vpc-id "
Creating development internet gateway (unattached)... { "InternetGateway": { "InternetGatewayId": "igw-2c3d4e5f6g7h8i9j", "Attachments": [], "Tags": [{ "Key": "Name", "Value": "anihpj-dev-igw" }], "OwnerId": "123456789012" } } ✓ Development IGW created: igw-2c3d4e5f6g7h8i9j ✓ Status: Unattached (ready for VPC assignment) igw-2c3d4e5f6g7h8i9j anihpj-dev-igw Next step: Attach to development VPC when needed
# Create development IGW (unattached) from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost from django.utils import timezone dev_igw = { 'igw_id': 'igw-2c3d4e5f6g7h8i9j', 'environment': 'Development', 'attached': False, 'purpose': 'Temporary testing and development' } print(f"Development Internet Gateway Created") print(f"IGW ID: {dev_igw['igw_id']}") print(f"Status: Unattached") print(f"Environment: Development") print(f"") # Development workflow dev_workflow = { 'scenario_1': 'Local development → SSH → EC2', 'scenario_2': 'Test environment branch → auto-attach IGW', 'scenario_3': 'Feature testing → temporary VPC → attach IGW', 'scenario_4': 'Integration test → clean up → detach IGW' } print(f"Development Use Cases:") for scenario, description in dev_workflow.items(): print(f" {scenario}: {description}") print(f"") # Multiple development environments dev_environments = { 'feature_branches': 8, 'active_dev_vpcs': 3, 'available_igws': 5, 'shared_igw_pool': True } print(f"Development Resource Pool:") for resource, value in dev_environments.items(): print(f" {resource}: {value}") # Attachment process attachment_process = { 'step_1': 'Create VPC for new feature branch', 'step_2': 'Attach pre-created IGW to VPC', 'step_3': 'Configure route tables', 'step_4': 'Launch EC2 instances', 'step_5': 'Test feature (10 days)', 'step_6': 'Detach IGW and delete VPC' } print(f"\nFeature Branch Workflow:") for step, action in attachment_process.items(): print(f" {step}: {action}") # Cost optimization for dev cost_optimization = { 'igw_cost': '$0 (no hourly charge)', 'vpc_cost': '$0 (no hourly charge)', 'instances_scheduled': 'Off outside business hours', 'storage_cleanup': 'Weekly (delete snapshots)', 'budget_alert': '$100/month (all dev combined)' } print(f"\nCost Optimization (Development):") for item, value in cost_optimization.items(): print(f" {item}: {value}") # Django testing benefits django_benefits = { 'network_isolation': 'Each feature gets own VPC', 'quick_testing': 'Deploy and test in < 5 minutes', 'tear_down': 'Delete resources after testing', 'no_prod_impact': 'Completely isolated from production', 'cost_controlled': 'Automatic cleanup' } print(f"\nDjango Testing Benefits:") for benefit, description in django_benefits.items(): print(f" {benefit}: {description}") # Attachment commands for later use attach_commands = { 'list_unattached': 'aws ec2 describe-internet-gateways --filters "Name=attachment.state,Values=available"', 'attach_to_vpc': 'aws ec2 attach-internet-gateway --igw-id igw-2c3d4e5f6g7h8i9j --vpc-id ', 'detach_from_vpc': 'aws ec2 detach-internet-gateway --igw-id igw-2c3d4e5f6g7h8i9j --vpc-id ', 'delete_igw': 'aws ec2 delete-internet-gateway --igw-id igw-2c3d4e5f6g7h8i9j' } print(f"\nManagement Commands (for later use):") for command_name, command in attach_commands.items(): print(f" {command_name}:") print(f" {command}") print(f"\n✓ Development IGW created (unattached)") print(f"✓ Ready to attach when needed")
Example 4: Create Multi-Region IGW Pool for Disaster Recovery

Create internet gateways in multiple regions for a disaster recovery setup, allowing quick failover to alternate regions.

#!/bin/bash
# Create multi-region IGW pool for DR
echo "Creating multi-region Internet Gateway pool..."
echo ""

declare -A regions=(
  ["us-east-1"]="vpc-0a1b2c3d4e5f6g7h8"
  ["eu-west-1"]="vpc-1b2c3d4e5f6g7h8i9"
  ["ap-southeast-1"]="vpc-2c3d4e5f6g7h8i9j0"
)

declare -A igw_ids

for region in "${!regions[@]}"; do
  vpc_id="${regions[$region]}"
  igw_name="anihpj-igw-$region"
  
  echo "Creating IGW in $region..."
  
  igw_id=$(aws ec2 create-internet-gateway \
    --tag-specifications "ResourceType=internet-gateway,Tags=[
      {Key=Name,Value=$igw_name},
      {Key=Environment,Value=Production},
      {Key=Region,Value=$region},
      {Key=DisasterRecovery,Value=Enabled}
    ]" \
    --region $region \
    --query 'InternetGateway.InternetGatewayId' \
    --output text)
  
  igw_ids[$region]=$igw_id
  echo "  ✓ Created: $igw_id"
  
  # Attach to VPC
  aws ec2 attach-internet-gateway \
    --internet-gateway-id $igw_id \
    --vpc-id $vpc_id \
    --region $region
  
  echo "  ✓ Attached to VPC: $vpc_id"
  echo ""
done

echo "Multi-Region IGW Pool Created:"
for region in "${!igw_ids[@]}"; do
  echo "  Region: $region → IGW: ${igw_ids[$region]}"
done
echo ""

echo "Disaster Recovery Configuration:"
echo "  Primary Region: us-east-1"
echo "  Secondary Region: eu-west-1"
echo "  Tertiary Region: ap-southeast-1"
echo "  RTO: < 5 minutes (DNS failover)"
echo "  RPO: < 1 minute (multi-region replication)"
Creating multi-region Internet Gateway pool... Creating IGW in us-east-1... { "InternetGateway": { "InternetGatewayId": "igw-0a1b2c3d4e5f6g7h", "Attachments": [], "Tags": [{ "Key": "Name", "Value": "anihpj-igw-us-east-1" }], "OwnerId": "123456789012" } } ✓ Created: igw-0a1b2c3d4e5f6g7h ✓ Attached to VPC: vpc-0a1b2c3d4e5f6g7h8 Creating IGW in eu-west-1... ✓ Created: igw-1b2c3d4e5f6g7h8i ✓ Attached to VPC: vpc-1b2c3d4e5f6g7h8i9 Creating IGW in ap-southeast-1... ✓ Created: igw-2c3d4e5f6g7h8i9j ✓ Attached to VPC: vpc-2c3d4e5f6g7h8i9j0 Multi-Region IGW Pool Created: Region: us-east-1 → IGW: igw-0a1b2c3d4e5f6g7h Region: eu-west-1 → IGW: igw-1b2c3d4e5f6g7h8i Region: ap-southeast-1 → IGW: igw-2c3d4e5f6g7h8i9j
# Create multi-region Internet Gateway pool from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost from django.utils import timezone multi_region_igws = { 'primary': { 'region': 'us-east-1', 'igw_id': 'igw-0a1b2c3d4e5f6g7h', 'vpc_id': 'vpc-0a1b2c3d4e5f6g7h8', 'status': 'Active' }, 'secondary': { 'region': 'eu-west-1', 'igw_id': 'igw-1b2c3d4e5f6g7h8i', 'vpc_id': 'vpc-1b2c3d4e5f6g7h8i9', 'status': 'Standby' }, 'tertiary': { 'region': 'ap-southeast-1', 'igw_id': 'igw-2c3d4e5f6g7h8i9j', 'vpc_id': 'vpc-2c3d4e5f6g7h8i9j0', 'status': 'Standby' } } print(f"Multi-Region Internet Gateway Pool") print(f"=" * 60) print(f"") for role, igw_info in multi_region_igws.items(): print(f"{role.upper()} - {igw_info['region']}") print(f" IGW ID: {igw_info['igw_id']}") print(f" VPC ID: {igw_info['vpc_id']}") print(f" Status: {igw_info['status']}") print(f"") # Global load balancing setup global_lb = { 'dns_service': 'Route 53', 'routing_policy': 'Geolocation + Failover', 'health_checks': 'CloudWatch + ALB', 'failover_trigger': 'Primary region ALB unhealthy' } print(f"Global Load Balancing:") for setting, value in global_lb.items(): print(f" {setting}: {value}") print(f"") # Data replication strategy replication = { 'database': 'Multi-region RDS (MySQL)', 'replication_lag': '< 1 second (DMS)', 'cache': 'ElastiCache Global Datastore', 'static_files': 'S3 (cross-region replication)', 'user_sessions': 'DynamoDB (global tables)' } print(f"Data Replication Strategy:") for component, config in replication.items(): print(f" {component}: {config}") #
